item.title: AngularJS expression injection - eval expression 1.4.0 to 1.4.9
item.description:
Detects AngularJS client-side template and expression injection by sending version-gated AngularJS sandbox-escape probes to query and form parameters, then requiring browser-executed proof from the PTK browser-nav harness.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Avoid eval with string literals
item.description:
Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Avoid execScript dynamic execution
item.description:
Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Avoid Function constructor with strings
item.description:

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Credit Card Number
item.description: Sensitive data is anything that should not be accessible to admin access, known as sensitive data. Sensitive data may include personally identifiable information (PII), such as Social Security numbers, financial information, or login credentials. Sensitive Data Exposure occurs when an organization unknowingly exposes sensitive data or when a security incident leads to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, or admin disclosure of, or access to sensitive data.

item.title: Disallow direct document.cookie assignment (incl. bracket access)
item.description:
Detects cases where attacker-controlled DOM data is written into cookies (document.cookie or common wrapper functions). Can indicate session fixation, logic control, or preparation for exploit-chains.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow direct navigation primitives
item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow document.write()/writeln()
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow innerHTML/outerHTML assignments
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Disallow insertAdjacentHTML()

Detects dynamic execution of attacker-controlled strings in JavaScript sinks such as eval(), Function(), string-based timers, execScript, or script.text assignments. Exploiting these flows lets attackers execute arbitrary JS without relying on HTML injection.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM-based Link Manipulation (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects DOM code that rewrites link destinations (href attributes) with attacker-controlled data. Manipulated links can mislead users into visiting malicious targets even if navigation is not forced automatically.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM-based Open Redirection (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM-based XSS (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Dynamic ACAO without Vary: Origin
item.description:
Adds passive CORS posture checks: missing Vary: Origin for dynamic ACAO, and permissive allowed headers/methods.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Dynamic AngularJS $compile/$interpolate template
item.description:
Finds AngularJS code patterns where untrusted data is compiled or parsed as AngularJS expressions/templates, including $parse, $interpolate, $compile, interpolation delimiters and ng-* expression attributes.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Exposure of Git repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..

item.title: Exposure of Mercurial repository
item.description: Version control repositories such as CVS or git store version-specific metadata and other details within subdirectories. If these subdirectories are stored on a web server or added to an archive, then these could be used by an attacker. This information may include usernames, filenames, path root, IP addresses, and detailed ‘diff’ data about how files have been changed - which could reveal source code snippets that were never intended to be made public..

item.title: Social Security Number
item.description: Sensitive data is anything that should not be accessible to admin access, known as sensitive data. Sensitive data may include personally identifiable information (PII), such as Social Security numbers, financial information, or login credentials. Sensitive Data Exposure occurs when an organization unknowingly exposes sensitive data or when a security incident leads to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, or admin disclosure of, or access to sensitive data.

Flags high-value endpoint patterns observed in traffic (admin panels, debug endpoints, consoles, and backup/config file paths).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: SQL Injection - Double Quote (after)
item.description: A SQL injection attack consists of insertion or injection of a SQL query via the input data from the client to the application. A successful SQL injection exploit can read sensitive data from the database, modify database data (Insert/Update/Delete), execute administration operations on the database (such as shutdown the DBMS), recover the content of a given file present on the DBMS file system and in some cases issue commands to the operating system. SQL injection attacks are a type of injection attack, in which SQL commands are injected into data-plane input in order to affect the execution of predefined SQL commands.

item.title: SSRF / webhook URL candidate parameter
item.description:
Flags request parameters and JSON keys commonly associated with high-impact findings (open redirect, SSRF, IDOR, file/path traversal).

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Strict-Transport-Security sent over HTTP (ineffective)
item.description:
The OWASP Secure Headers Project describes HTTP response headers that your application can use to increase the security of your application. Once set, these HTTP response headers can restrict modern browsers from running into easily preventable vulnerabilities.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Stripe publishable key exposed
item.description:
Flags secret-like tokens and exposed configuration values in observed HTML/JS/JSON responses. These are recon leads; validate sensitivity before reporting.

item.title: Tainted data compiled as AngularJS template
item.description:
Finds AngularJS code patterns where untrusted data is compiled or parsed as AngularJS expressions/templates, including $parse, $interpolate, $compile, interpolation delimiters and ng-* expression attributes.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.description:

item.title: Template injection (taint flow)

Detects dynamic client-side template compilation/rendering where attacker-controlled templates or outputs are injected into the DOM.

item.title: Template output injected into DOM
item.description:
Detects dynamic client-side template compilation/rendering where attacker-controlled templates or outputs are injected into the DOM.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: template.innerHTML with dynamic content
item.description:
Detects cases where untrusted data from the DOM (URL, element values, storage, messages, etc.) flows into HTML/JS execution sinks (e.g., innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, string-based setTimeout, insertAdjacentHTML) without proper sanitization or encoding \u2014 enabling DOM-based cross-site scripting.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Untrusted DOM data into createHTMLDocument
item.description:
Detects untrusted DOM data being written into form metadata (formAction/target/method/value/placeholder), inline style surfaces (style/cssText/background*), document.title, history state, or createHTMLDocument — mutations that influence UI/navigation state without covering classic href/src/action sinks already handled elsewhere.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Untrusted DOM data into navigation-adjacent sinks
item.description:

item.title: Untrusted DOM data into UI mutation sinks
item.description:

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Web Message Injection (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects unsafe postMessage usage and message event handling issues (missing origin validation, wildcard targetOrigin, tainted data flowing into DOM/code sinks).

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: Webpack dev-server / hot reload artifacts
item.description:
Detects source map references and common debug artifacts in observed HTML/JS responses. These are high-value recon leads for code disclosure and hidden endpoints.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Wildcard reply from message handler
item.description:

item.title: XSS - attribute context img onerror
item.description: Reflected Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is another name for non-persistent XSS, where the attack doesn’t load with the vulnerable web application but is originated by the victim loading the offending URI. In this article we will see some ways to test a web application for this kind of vulnerability.
